title: Rich Charred French Carrot Tarte Tatin
description: A charred, creamy, and adventurous French dish from Brittany, featuring ground ginger and a unique nigiri-shaping technique, this tarte tatin is a shojin-friendly delight.

ingredients.name: carrots
ingredients.notes: peeled and sliced into 1/4 inch thick rounds

instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, sugar, and salt.
Add the melted butter, eggs, heavy cream, ground ginger, and chopped thyme to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Roll out the puff pastry on a floured surface to a large rectangle, about 1/4 inch thick.
In a large skillet, caramelize the sliced carrots over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until they are charred and tender.
Arrange the caramelized carrots in a circular pattern on the puff pastry, leaving a 1 inch border around the edges.
Spoon the dough mixture over the carrots, making sure to cover them evenly.
Fold the edges of the puff pastry up over the filling, pressing gently to seal.
Place the tarte tatin on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 35 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown.
Remove the tarte tatin from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
